behavior_summary: U8P_PEDESTAL::look is a U8P_PEDESTAL::look-type process that runs when the player examines the PEDESTAL object and presents a single short description ('pedestal ').
After emitting the bark text the process appears to suspend/terminate; there is no branching, menu, or flag manipulation.
No external calls or global flags are read or written by this script excerpt.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: entry → emit Bark('pedestal ') → suspend/terminate.
No conditional branches, menus, or loops present.
Function is implemented as a process (processType 0x0208) and is triggered by an inspect/U8P_PEDESTAL::look action on the object.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text 'pedestal ' intentional (formatting) or an artifact of extraction?
Is the suspend/termination explicit in the source or inferred from the process structure (behavior inferred with high confidence but the raw node was unknown)?
Are there any other event handlers (e.g., use, enterFastArea) for this object in other files that change behavior beyond this U8P_PEDESTAL::look handler?

main_function.name: U8P_PEDESTAL::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text when the player examines the pedestal; present a single bark and end the U8P_PEDESTAL::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player 'U8P_PEDESTAL::look' / examine action on the PEDESTAL object

function_map.name: U8P_PEDESTAL::look
function_map.role: Handle the object's examine/U8P_PEDESTAL::look action by emitting a short bark ('pedestal ') and then suspending/ending the process.
